ucs.title: [LLM] DarkHydrus RogueRobin PowerShell DNS-tunneling C2 (TXT-record beaconing)
ucs.description: Hunts DarkHydrus's signature RogueRobin backdoor, a PowerShell payload that exfils and beacons over DNS TXT queries to actor-controlled subdomains. We look for powershell.exe (or wscript/regsvr32 spawning PS) generating high-volume, high-entropy DNS lookups — the exact pattern Unit 42 documented for DarkHydrus's Middle East government/edu campaigns.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Traffic.dest) as dest values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as dest_port values(All_Traffic.bytes_out) as bytes_out from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_port=53 OR All_Traffic.app=dns by All_Traffic.src host All_Traffic.process_name _time span=10m | rename All_Traffic.* as * | search process_name IN ("powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","pwsh.exe") | stats sum(count) as dns_query_count dc(dest) as unique_resolvers sum(bytes_out) as total_bytes by src process_name | where dns_query_count > 200 AND total_bytes > 50000 | join type=outer src process_name [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.process) as cmdline from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") (Processes.process="*-w hidden*" OR Processes.process="*-WindowStyle Hidden*" OR Processes.process="*FromBase64String*" OR Processes.process="*Resolve-DnsName*" OR Processes.process="*nslookup*") by Processes.dest Processes.process_name | rename Processes.dest as src Processes.process_name as process_name] | where isnotnull(cmdline) | sort - dns_query_count
ucs.kql: let suspiciousPS = DeviceProcessEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","pwsh.exe") | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-w hidden","-WindowStyle Hidden","-enc","FromBase64String","Resolve-DnsName","nslookup","DownloadString") | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, PSPid=ProcessId, PSCmd=ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName; let dnsBeacon = DeviceNetworkEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where ActionType == "DnsQueryResponse" or RemotePort == 53 | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") | summarize QueryCount=count(), DistinctNames=dcount(RemoteUrl), Sample=make_set(RemoteUrl, 25), TotalBytes=sum(toint(coalesce(tostring(AdditionalFields),"0"))) by DeviceId, InitiatingProcessId, bin(Timestamp, 1h) | where QueryCount > 150 and DistinctNames > 50; dnsBeacon | join kind=inner suspiciousPS on $left.DeviceId == $right.DeviceId and $left.InitiatingProcessId == $right.PSPid | extend SubdomainEntropy = array_length(Sample) | project Timestamp, DeviceName, PSCmd, QueryCount, DistinctNames, TotalBytes, Sample | order by QueryCount desc
ucs.rationale: DarkHydrus's RogueRobin (and its later Google Drive variant) is uniquely characterized by PowerShell-driven DNS TXT-record tunneling — Unit 42's July 2018 report documented the specific pattern of PowerShell.exe issuing thousands of TXT lookups to randomized actor subdomains. Random PowerShell + random DNS volume is noisy; the AND of (hidden/encoded PowerShell process) ∧ (the same PID generating h

ucs.title: [LLM] DarkHydrus Phishery template-injection forced-auth credential harvest
ucs.description: Hunts DarkHydrus's documented use of the open-source Phishery tool: a spearphished Office document containing a remote template reference (T1221) that, when opened, forces Word/Excel to authenticate to an attacker-controlled URL — leaking NTLM hashes or prompting for plaintext creds. This is the actor's known initial-access pattern against Middle East government targets.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Traffic.dest) as dest values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as dest_port values(All_Traffic.url) as url values(All_Traffic.user) as user from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe") (All_Traffic.dest_port IN (80,443,445,139) OR All_Traffic.app IN ("http","https","smb")) by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.process_name | rename All_Traffic.* as * | search NOT (dest IN ("*.microsoft.com","*.office.com","*.office365.com","*.sharepoint.com","*.officeapps.live.com","*.windows.net")) | join type=inner src [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process | rename Processes.dest as src Processes.parent_process_name as parent Processes.process as office_cmd | search parent IN ("outlook.exe","explorer.exe")] | stats values(url) as urls values(dest) as dests values(dest_port) as ports values(office_cmd) as office_cmd by src process_name | where mvcount(urls) > 0
ucs.kql: let officeChildren = DeviceProcessEvents | where Timestamp > ago(14d) | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe") | where InitiatingProcessParentFileName in~ ("outlook.exe","explorer.exe","olk.exe"); let officeOutbound = DeviceNetworkEvents | where Timestamp > ago(14d) | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe") | where RemotePort in (80,443,445,139) | where not(RemoteUrl has_any ("microsoft.com","office.com","office365.com","sharepoint.com","officeapps.live.com","windows.net","digicert.com","verisign.com")) | where not(ipv4_is_private(RemoteIP)) | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessId, RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, RemotePort, ActionType; let suspiciousFiles = DeviceFileEvents | where Timestamp > ago(14d) | where FileName endswith ".docx" or FileName endswith ".dotx" or FileName endswith ".xlsx" or FileName endswith ".rtf" or FileName endswith ".iqy" | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("outlook.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe") | project FileTimestamp=Timestamp, DeviceId, DroppedFile=FileName, FolderPath; officeOutbound | join kind=inner officeChildren on DeviceId, $left.InitiatingProcessId == $right.ProcessId | join kind=leftouter suspiciousFiles on DeviceId | where Timestamp between ((FileTimestamp) .. (FileTimestamp + 1h)) or isempty(FileTimestamp) | project Timestamp, DeviceName, DroppedFile, OfficeApp=InitiatingProcessFileName, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemotePort, ParentProcess=InitiatingProcessParentFileName | union (EmailAttachmentInfo | where Timestamp > ago(14d) | where FileType in~ ("docx","dotx","rtf","iqy") | join kind=inner (EmailEvents | where Timestamp > ago(14d) | where SenderFromDomain !endswith "<your-org>.com") on NetworkMessageId | project Timestamp, RecipientEmailAddress, SenderFromAddress, Subject, FileName, SHA256) | sort by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: DarkHydrus is one of the few named groups whose tradecraft expressly chains Phishery-style template injection with forced SMB/HTTP authentication against Middle East government/edu targets (Unit 42 Dec 2017 Playbook, Jul 2018 report). The detection's discriminator is the AND of (Outlook→Office app spawn) ∧ (Office app reaching out over 80/443/445/139 to non-Microsoft infrastructure) shortly after
